items.title: Workers to escalate strike at Inpex's Ichthys LNG sites after talks fail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Workers will escalate the strike at Inpex's Ichthys LNG facilities in Australia that started last week over a wage dispute, after talks failed to progress with the Japanese operator, the Offshore Alliance union said on Monday. The Offshore Alliance, made up of the Maritime Union of Australia and the Australian Workers' Union, said it would ramp up the four-hour stoppages and bans at the facilities which commenced last Wednesday. The union said Inpex, Japan's biggest oil and gas explorer, reneged on terms agreed in a Fair Work Commission-facilitated bargaining that took place on Saturday.

items.title: 7.8-magnitude quake off Philippines kills at least one, sparks tsunami alerts
items.summary: MANILA: A 7.8-magnitude earthquake that struck the southern Philippines on Monday killed at least one person and collapsed buildings, police said, as the disaster sparked tsunami warnings across the region. It has really collapsed! The building has really collapsed!"

items.title: Seven OPEC+ countries to raise oil output in July
items.summary: VIENNA: The Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) and its allies, known as OPEC+, on Sunday decided to increase oil output by 188,000 barrels per day in July, according to an OPEC statement, reported Xinhua. The reason of the upward production adjustment by the seven OPEC+ countries was the "collective commitment to support oil market stability", the statement said. According to the statement, the seven countries would retain full flexibility to adjust their production depending on evolving market conditions.

items.title: Zahid dismisses ‘political drama’ claims, says Johor assembly dissolution returns mandate to people for stability
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 8 — Barisan Nasional (BN) Chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has dismissed claims by certain quarters who characterised the move to dissolve the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 1, as a ‘political drama’. The BN Chairman said this during his speech at the Launching Ceremony of the BN Machinery for the 16th Johor State Election here yesterday. Also present were Johor BN Chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, MCA President Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong, MIC President Tan Sri Dr SA Vigneswaran, and People’s Progressive Party (PPP) President Datuk Dr J Loga Bala Mohan.

items.title: Adoptive mother arrested after girl, 9, forced to live in storeroom behind Seremban home, police say
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — A nine-year-old girl was allegedly confined to a storeroom behind her family’s home in Seremban for weeks, fed only twice a day and subjected to physical abuse, police said today. Seremban district police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Yatim Osman said preliminary investigations found the child had been made to stay in an unlocked storeroom behind the house throughout the school holidays and was allowed into the house only to use the toilet. Mohd Yatim said police received a report on the incident at 7.22pm yesterday before launching an investigation.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues morning storm warning for Perak, Selangor, Sarawak, Sabah and Labuan until 11am
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds across four states and Labuan until 11am today. In a statement today, MetMalaysia said the weather alert covers Perak, involving Manjung, Perak Tengah, Bagan Datuk and Hilir Perak, as well as Sabak Bernam in Selangor. The same warning was also issued for Sarawak, covering Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Julau and Meradong), Sibu, Mukah (Daro, Matu, Dalat and Mukah) and Kapit (Song), while in Sabah it involves the interior area of Kuala Penyu.

items.title: Mindanao quake felt in Sabah, MetMalaysia monitoring situation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tremors were felt in Tawau, Semporna and surrounding areas following a strong offshore earthquake that struck Mindanao, Philippines, at 7.37am today. In Indonesia, the Meteorology, Climatology and Geophysics Agency (BMKG) is also tracking the seismic event closely, with early reports indicating tremors may have been felt in parts of eastern Indonesia, including Sulawesi and nearby regions. BMKG said it is evaluating the potential tsunami impact along Indonesia's eastern coastline as a precautionary measure.

items.title: 'Iskandar Affan was excited about the new car' - Aunt recalls final moments before crash
items.summary: Norzamilah Ghazali, who is the aunt of Iskandar Affan Ibrahim, said the child was having a meal when he heard the sports utility vehicle (SUV) arrive at his father Ibrahim Ghazali's house in Taman Bertam, Penang. Those killed at the scene were Iskandar's grandmother Nora Mhd Husin, 55; his uncles Ahmad Shafiq Ahmad Shukri, 29, who was driving, and Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21; his aunt Jamaliah Sannusi, 29, who was Ahmad Shafiq's wife; and their two-month-old baby, Ahmad Mikail Ahmad Shafiq. Norzamilah said Nora was the mother-in-law of her 39-year-old brother, Ibrahim.

items.title: Kedah police chief slams viral rumours, urges space for probe into Sungai Petani SUV-lorry collision
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 8 — Police reminded members of the public to not speculate about the incident that claimed the lives of six family members during a tragic accident in Pinang Tunggal, near Sungai Petani, yesterday. “The SUV, carrying seven family members including a police officer, was heading towards Sungai Petani on their way to Merbok to visit the graves of other family members, while the lorry, driven by a 49-year-old local man, was heading towards Tikam Batu. The six who died in the tragic accident were identified as: Ahmad Syafiq Ahmad Shukri, 29, Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21, Jamaliah Sannusi, 29, Nora Mohd Husin, 55, Iskandar Affan Ibrahim, 7 and Ahmad Mikail Ahmad Syafiq, 2 months, while the injured victim is Aulia Sofea Ahmad Syafiq, 3.

items.title: Japan's economy cools on weak capex in Q1
items.summary: The GDP figures released by the Cabinet Office showed the economy expanded an annualised 1.8 per cent in the first quarter, compared with the initially estimated 2.1 per cent. Economists' median forecast was for 1.3 per cent growth. Without annualisation, GDP grew 0.5 per cent, slightly better than the median forecast for a 0.3 per cent expansion and matching the preliminary figure.

items.title: UK firms pause hiring as Iran war stings
items.summary: LONDON: Britain's jobs market cooled rapidly in May after employers put the brakes on permanent hiring after the Iran war heightened cost pressures and uncertainty about the economic outlook, an industry survey showed on Monday. The monthly Report on Jobs from accountants KPMG and the Recruitment and Employment Confederation, a trade body, showed permanent job placements fell at the fastest pace since July 2025. REC and KPMG also said: * Permanent staff placements fell to 44.1 in May, down sharply from 47.5 in April * Firms ramped up hiring of temporary staff, with the index rising to 52.2 from 50.4, expanding at the quickest pace since April 2023, as firms opted for short-term staff due to uncertainty about the Iran war * The availability of candidates for permanent and temporary roles also rose faster than in April * Vacancies dropped at the sharpest pace since February, driven by a sharper fall in vacancies for permanent roles * The survey was based on responses from a panel of around 400 recruitment agencies between May 12 and May 22 © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Tiny homes see tiny boost for Los Angeles homeless as World Cup nears
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: For the past few months, Michael Gilpin has slept in a small, prefabricated house he moved into when he got off the streets of Los Angeles as part of a major drive to reduce homelessness. Gilpin is one of a few thousand people who have benefited from hundreds of millions of dollars spent to find people a place to live in Los Angeles. Visitors to Los Angeles are frequently struck by the staggering levels of homelessness in a city that also has pockets of astonishing wealth.

items.title: Five injured in stabbing at New York's Penn Station
items.summary: NEW YORK: Five people were wounded Sunday in a stabbing at New York's Penn Station, according to the city fire department, which said all victims were transported to hospital and the suspect was detained. The victims were all civilians, including one person with serious injuries, two with moderate injuries and two with minor injuries, the New York Fire Department said in a brief statement. Madison Square Garden, located directly above Penn Station in downtown Manhattan, will host games three and four of the NBA Finals on Monday and Wednesday between the New York Knicks and the San Antonio Spurs.

items.title: Excited boy’s first ride in uncle’s brand‑new Proton X50 ends in Sungai Petani tragedy
items.summary: A family member, Ibrahim Ghazali, 39, said the vehicle, a Proton X50, belonged to his brother-in-law Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21, but was being driven by Ahmad Fahim’s brother, Ahmad Shafiq, 27, at the time of the accident. Ibrahim said his son, Iskandar Arfan, seven, was travelling with Ahmad Fahim, Ahmad Shafiq and four others to Merbok near here to visit the grave of his late father-in-law when the accident occurred. Ahmad Fahim, Ahmad Shafiq and Iskandar Arfan died after the SUV collided with a lorry at about 3.45pm, a crash that also claimed the lives of Ibrahim’s mother-in-law, Nora Mhd Husin, 55; Jamaliah Sannusi, 27; and a six-month-old baby whose identity and gender have yet to be confirmed.

items.title: #NSTviral: 'Unsung heroes': Praise pours in for SAR teams in Gunung Batu Putih rescue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The dedication and sacrifice of rescue teams and volunteers involved in the search-and-rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, in Gunung Batu Putih, Perak, have drawn praise online, with social media users hailing their efforts as heroic and selfless. Joint coordinator of the Malim Gunung SAR team, Muzafar Mohamad, who shared his experience in a Facebook post, said the operation, which lasted several days across dense forest and hilly terrain, involved significant risks, with some rescuers nearly losing their lives during the mission. He said several volunteers faced dangerous situations while carrying out search efforts based on plans coordinated at the incident control post.

items.title: Muhyiddin reaffirms Bersatu-PAS ties, says party to consider Perikatan proposals
items.summary: The Bersatu president said he had received a letter from PAS secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan dated June 6 regarding relations between the two parties and proposals aimed at reinforcing the Opposition coalition. “I have received a letter from the PAS secretary-general dated June 6, 2026, regarding the relationship between Bersatu and PAS as well as proposals to strengthen PN. The exchange comes after PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said that the party was reviewing its cooperation with Bersatu.

items.title: 'I felt I was going to lose him': Mother's premonition before crash killed son, five relatives
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: A mother's premonition of losing her son came true in the most devastating way when her seven-year-old was killed alongside five family members on Sunday. Atikah, the mother of Iskandar Affan Ibrahim, said she felt an unexplainable sense of impending loss in the days leading up to the horrific road crash. Arikah said she also noticed subtle changes in Iskandar's behaviour before the incident, as if he was preparing to go somewhere.

items.title: Johor showdown looms as Zahid unveils BN machinery, veterans return and youth army marches in
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 8 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will finalise its full list of candidates for the Johor State Election in the near future. Ahmad Zahid said this during a press conference after officiating the Launching Ceremony of the Johor State BN Election Machinery at the EduCity Sports Complex Indoor Stadium, here yesterday. Previously, Johor BN announced that it would contest all 56 State Legislative Assembly (DUN) seats in the upcoming state election.

items.title: First of six family members killed in tragic Sungai Petani crash laid to rest
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Seven-year-old Iskandar Affan Ibrahim, who was among six victims killed in a horrific road accident yesterday, was laid to rest early today. Iskandar's remains were later brought to Masjid Permatang Kuang Penaga for funeral prayers. Besides Iskandar, those killed were Ahmad Syafiq Ahmad Shukri, 29; Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21; Jamaliah Sannusi, 29; Nora Mohd Husin, 55; and two-month-old Ahmad Mikail Ahmad Syafiq.

items.title: Oil prices rise more than $2 on Israel strikes on Lebanon
items.summary: BEIJING: Oil prices were up more than US$2 a barrel on Monday after Israel on Sunday launched renewed strikes on Lebanon despite a truce between the two countries, eroding hopes for an end to the wider war and a restart to crude flows through the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. crude futures were up US$2.10, or 2.32 per cent, at US$92.64 per barrel as of 0013 GMT, while Brent crude futures rose US$2.33, or 2.5 per cent, to US$95.42 a barrel. Iran retaliated for the Beirut strikes on its ally Hezbollah by launching missiles at Israel.

items.title: Asia markets tumble as tech rout deepens
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Asian markets plunged on Monday as investors slammed the brakes on the red-hot AI rally, while Israeli strikes on Beirut sent oil prices and the dollar higher. An 8 per cent drop for South Korea's chip-heavy KOSPI benchmark triggered a 20-minute trading halt and has it down almost 17 per cent from last week's record high. Japan's Nikkei fell 3.5 per cent in early trade, though U.S. S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures made small gains.

items.title: [UPDATED] Perkeso flagged Daya Kerjaya 2.0 irregularities before MACC probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resources Ministry has clarified that suspected irregularities involving claims under the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 Programme were first detected by the Social Security Organisation's (Perkeso) internal anti-fraud mechanisms before the case was referred to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan said the ministry fully supports MACC's ongoing investigation and remains committed to ensuring government assistance programmes are administered with the highest standards of integrity, accountability and good governance. "Following the initial detection, AFEI launched intelligence-gathering efforts, surveillance and comprehensive investigations to verify the findings and determine the extent of the suspected abuse involving claims under the employment incentive programme," Ramanan said in a statement today.

items.title: Japan issues tsunami advisory after Philippines quake
items.summary: TOKYO: Japanese authorities issued a tsunami advisory on Monday along swathes of its Pacific coast after a major earthquake off the Philippines. The Japan Meteorological Agency said that tsunamis of up to one metre were projected to hit different regions from 11.30am. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: [UPDATED] Magnitude 7.8 quake hits Mindanao, tsunami warning issued
items.summary: MINDANAO: Tsunami warnings were issued after an earthquake of magnitude 7.8 struck off Mindanao in the southern Philippines on Monday, the German Research Centre for Geosciences said. The quake struck at a depth of 10km, GFZ said. The geophysics agencies of the Philippines and neighbouring Indonesia issued tsunami warnings.

items.title: Powerful 8.2-magnitude quake off Mindanao rattles southern Philippines, tremors felt in Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — A powerful earthquake struck near Mindanao in the southern Philippines this morning, with tremors felt in parts of Sabah. Malaysia’s Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the earthquake occurred at 7.37am, with a magnitude of 7.9 and a depth of 50km. International monitoring agencies reported varying preliminary measurements.

items.title: Peru exit polls show Fujimori ahead in ultra-tight presidential runoff
items.summary: LIMA: Voting stations closed Sunday in Peru's presidential election, with exit polls showing four-time candidate Keiko Fujimori with the slenderest of leads over her leftist rival. Pollsters at Ipsos and other firms said Fujimori led Roberto Sanchez by about one per centage point – 50.7 per cent versus 49.3 per cent – a statistical tie. Every vote counts," Fujimori said.

items.title: Plantation earnings outlook brightens after soft Q1
items.summary: Analysts said industry players could benefit from firmer crude palm oil (CPO) prices, with potential upside from a possible El Niño weather event in the second half of 2026. Kenanga Research said a weaker seasonal output during Q1 was compounded by lower CPO prices amid higher-than-expected inventory levels. Khoo expects earnings to improve ahead as CPO prices recover, although downstream margins are likely to remain under pressure.

items.title: Malaysia buffered from global tensions by energy exporter status: Bank of America
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy remains relatively resilient despite rising global tensions that have driven up fuel, fertiliser and broader commodity prices, Bank of America Malaysia top executive said. The bank's country executive Gautam Puntambekar said the impact of recent global disruptions is not unique to Malaysia, but its position as a net energy exporter provides a structural buffer against external shocks. Despite these risks, Malaysia's macroeconomic indicators remain solid, supported by domestic demand and policy buffers.

items.title: Beyond petroleum, beyond politics
items.summary: ALBERTA accounts for roughly four-fifths of Canada's oil production and possesses one of the world's largest proven petroleum reserves. Yet the province's recurring sovereignty movement demonstrates a reality often overlooked in public debates: resource disputes are rarely about resources alone. For many observers, Alberta's growing sovereignty movement appears to be about oil.

items.title: Bursa outlook holds firm despite challenging five months
items.summary: Following the first-quarter earnings season, TA Securities, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd, MBSB Research and CGS International Securities Malaysia Sdn Bhd maintained their FBM KLCI targets. So far this year, FBM KLCI gained 13.5 points, or 0.81 per cent, rising to 1,683.26 from 1,669.76, after the strong late 2025 and early 2026 runs. In 2025, the index rose 2.89 per cent to close at 1,680.11 points from 1,632.87 points at the beginning of the year.

items.title: Trump: Netanyahu will have 'no choice' but to accept Iran deal
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will have no choice but to accept whatever deal the US negotiates with Iran, US President Donald Trump told the Financial Times in an interview published on Sunday, because Trump "calls the shots". "He won't have any choice," Trump was quoted as saying. --REUTERS © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Trump walks out of NBC interview after challenge over election claims
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump lasted through probing questions about his Iran war strategy and repeated weather disruptions during an interview with US broadcaster NBC – but angrily stormed out when confronted over his unfounded claims of election fraud. Have a good time," Trump told "Meet the Press" host Kristen Welker before standing up and walking out of the interview that aired Sunday. Trump asked at one point.

items.title: Rescue, not cost, was priority in Jaslinda SAR mission, says Fire Dept
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Fire and Rescue Department has not done a full costing of the search and rescue operation for hiker Jaslinda Saludin, as such assessments are not routinely conducted, said its director-general. "The priority was Jaslinda's rescue and the safety of the team, not the cost," Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad told the New Straits Times . © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Trump tells Netanyahu: Don't retaliate against Iran
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump said Sunday he will call Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to urge him not to retaliate against Iranian missile strikes on Israel, news outlet Axios reported. Israel had its strike and Iran had its strike. Hopefully Israel is not going to retaliate.

items.title: Hiker's experience and skill kept her alive' - Mountain guide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Jaslinda Saludin survived nearly two weeks in the jungle by drawing on her experience, conserving energy, staying close to water sources and remaining calm, an expert tracker said. Malim Gunung Malaysia Association president Muzafar Mohamad said Jaslinda's ultimate anchor, however, was her immense will to live and see her family and friends. He said unlike some hikers who might panic, exhaust themselves and collapse, Jaslinda, 49, rationed her strength.

items.title: Best if Pas leaves PN, say Bersatu leaders
items.summary: This, they said, is because PN needs to move quickly on state election preparations. "Pas has repeatedly said it wants to join hands with Umno and revive MN2.0. The source said Bersatu's grassroots also believed Pas should leave, as it had betrayed PN several times by conducting negotiations without discussing them within the coalition.

items.title: From safe seats to the battlefront: How DAP shuffles incumbents in tactic to win tougher seats
items.summary: Marina’s announcement, issued just two days before Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi dissolved the state legislative assembly, fueled speculation about growing friction within the state’s DAP chapter. In the letter, Marina purportedly explained her decision, including that she had been asked to vacate her Skudai seat help her party win Tiram, a Malay-majority seat and a known Umno stronghold. Liew Chin Tong winning Perling state seat (2022) Liew, the DAP national organising secretary, was already MP for Iskandar Puteri and did not hold a state seat at the time.

items.title: Food businesses in Kuala Lumpur aren’t facing shortages yet... but they’re preparing for them already
items.summary: But for many food businesses, sourcing the ingredients behind those everyday menu items is becoming increasingly challenging as costs fluctuate and supply becomes less predictable. Madu Wanita, owner of two ayam gepuk outlets, said the price of cabbage has gone up and is affecting operating margins slightly. The staff said broader disruptions affecting international shipping and import logistics have created uncertainty around supplies.

items.title: 'Action heroes' shine at NST cosplay contest
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The New Straits Times (NST) hosted an online cosplay competition in conjunction with the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair over the weekend. The champion was Azwan Adizar, 39, from Rawang, who portrayed Son Goku. Azwan, who won RM300, said he discovered the competition through NST's Facebook page.

items.title: Israel vows retaliation after Iran fires 11 missiles
items.summary: Air raid sirens sounded across large areas of northern and central Israel, including the cities of Haifa, Caesarea and Hadera, according to the military. Afterwards, Israeli military chief Lieutenant General Eyal Zamir vowed the military would "strike the enemy with force as soon as the green light is given", the military said. Responding to a request for details, the military said a total of 11 missiles had been fired from Iran.

items.title: Kedah pushes legal challenge after Court of Appeal rules against gambling licence freeze
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 7 — Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor is not giving up the state’s decision to stop issuing and renewing number-betting outlet licences, despite losing the case at the Court of Appeal. He said the state will pursue the legal battle all the way to the Federal Court, The Star reported today. On June 2, the Court of Appeal released its written grounds for a December ruling that Kedah could not impose a blanket ban on lottery and betting businesses solely on policy objections to gambling.

items.title: ‘We must be ready for GE’: Sabah Pakatan revamps line-up after AGM
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 7 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) Sabah has restructured its leadership lineup as part of efforts to strengthen the coalition’s operations and prepare for the next general election (GE), its chairman Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said. We also need to strengthen the operations of PH as we prepare for the next General Election,” he said. Mustapha, who is also PKR Sabah liaison chairman, will remain PH Sabah chairman, assisted by DAP Sabah chairman Datuk Phoong Jin Zhe and Amanah Sabah chairman Datuk Lahirul Latigu as deputy chairmen.

items.title: American tourist found safe after Pulau Tioman hike mishap, rescued via phone location
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 7 — An American tourist who was reported missing while hiking on Pulau Tioman was found safe last night after he shared his real-time location via mobile phone with rescue teams. Rompin district police chief Supt Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi said police received information about the missing man at about 5.30pm yesterday after he failed to return to his resort accommodation following an activity along the old Kampung Tekek-Kampung Juara trail. “The victim was found at 8.33pm in the Bukit Parang forest area, Kampung Tekek, in a safe condition and without any injuries.

items.title: Food poisoning scare hits Rawang care and tahfiz centre, 27 victims rushed for treatment
items.summary: Sungai Buloh police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said the victims, including elderly people aged between 62 and 84, experienced symptoms such as vomiting and diarrhoea. He said preliminary investigations found that the incident occurred after all the victims had consumed food obtained from outside sources on June 4. Mohd Hafiz said the MOH has opened an investigation paper regarding the case under the Food Act 1983 and the Prevention and Control of Infectious Diseases Act 1988.

items.title: Melaka firefighters rescue hawksbill turtle stuck in drain near Tanjung Kling motel, safely released into sea
items.summary: MELAKA, June 7 — A turtle weighing between 40 and 60 kilogrammes was rescued by the Melaka Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) after it was found trapped in a drain near a motel in Tanjung Kling here today. Melaka JBPM Public Relations officer Deputy Fire Superintendent II Mohd Hafidzatullah Rashid said the department received an emergency call at 11.33am, and a team of seven personnel from the Tangga Batu Fire and Rescue Station was deployed to the location. “Upon arrival, a turtle was found trapped in the drain, and a rescue operation was carried out successfully.

items.title: ‘Your president sits next to me every week’: Anthony Loke says after Onn Hafiz’s ‘won't sit at same table’ jab
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke fired back at Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chief Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi after the latter declared he would rather resign than cooperate with DAP in forming the next Johor state government. This is the spirit of Johor, Bangsa Johor”. While DAP and Umno are coalition partners in the unity government, Onn Hafiz has made clear that the arrangement does not extend to Johor.

items.title: ‘Sit at the same table with DAP’? Onn Hafiz says he’d rather give up Johor MB post
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 7 — Caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has declared he would not remain in office if Barisan Nasional (BN) were to form a state government with DAP. Free Malaysia Today (FMT) reported that the statement was made during the launch of BN’s election machinery in Iskandar Puteri, where he rejected suggestions that support for BN would translate into backing for DAP, describing the claim as “slander”. This is the spirit of Johor, the Bangsa Johor spirit,” he was quoted as saying.

items.title: Anwar: No fuel hikes, no borrowing for subsidies as govt absorbs billions
items.summary: On the contrary, Anwar said the government would continue to try to cover the cost of fuel subsidies by incurring large expenses every month to avoid burdening the people. Not in the old allocation, but when the price of oil rose, we paid RM5 billion, RM3 billion a month. He said if the government released RM3 billion every month for a period of 10 months, the total would reach RM30 billion.

items.title: Six in same family dead, toddler fighting for life after lorry collides with Proton X50 in Sungai Petani
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 7 — Six family members, including a six-month-old baby, were killed in a collision between a lorry and a Proton X50 along Kampung Baru Pinang Tunggal near here this afternoon. “The crash involved a 10-ton lorry with a Proton X50, with seven people onboard, including the baby. Four of the dead have been identified as Ahmad Shafiq Ahmad Shukri, 27; Nora Mhd Husin, 55; Jamaliah Sannusi, 27; and Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21.

items.title: Did a Myanmar national really get a blue MyKad? Police refer viral claim to NRD
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 7 — Police have referred a video regarding claims of an identity card issued to an alleged Myanmar national circulating on social media to the National Registration Department (NRD) for verification. Putrajaya police chief ACP Aidi Sham Mohamed said the case stems from a report received on June 5 at 8.45am. “Initial investigations found a viral Facebook video posted by the account ‘Na’am Ahmad’, which questioned how someone allegedly from Myanmar could possess a blue MyKad.

items.title: A year after Gerik bus crash, UPSI survivors still healing from emotional scars
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH, June 7 — Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI) students who survived a bus crash in Gerik, Perak, on June 9 last year are still recovering from their trauma, thanks to the continued support of their families, friends and the university. A crash survivor, Abdul Wafiy Kamaruddin, 24, said he has managed to deal with the trauma of using public transport, even though he had trouble being inside vehicles following his ordeal, thanks to the university’s continuous counselling support for survivors. “The UPSI counsellors always follow up on those of us who survived the crash and that really helped our emotional recovery,” he told reporters during a UPSI gathering for students and families involved in the crash in Tok Bali today.

items.title: Flood control, water security in focus as Malaysia and Thailand step up Sungai Golok cooperation
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 7 — The Thai Cabinet’s approval of the Sungai Golok Basin development project opens up avenues for closer cooperation between Malaysia and Thailand in addressing flood risks, water security and ecosystem restoration in the border areas of both countries. National Security Council (NSC) Director-General Datuk Raja Nushirwan Zainal Abidin said Malaysia also intends to implement related developments in the area, though the matter is still in the planning stages. The Thai Cabinet was previously reported to have approved a joint environmental project with Malaysia aimed at reducing flood risks, enhancing water security and restoring the ecosystem in the Sungai Golok Basin.

items.title: MACC uncovers ghost workers, forged documents in RM9m Daya Kerjaya 2.0 claims
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, June 7 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has uncovered irregularities involving the government’s Daya Kerjaya 2.0 programme, with false claims amounting to RM9.01 million. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said investigations, with an operation expected to begin next week, found that 143 companies involving 320 workers nationwide were linked to the alleged misuse of government funds under the Social Security Organisation’s (Perkeso) initiative in 2024 and 2025. He said the MACC had also identified another 1,638 companies suspected of engaging in similar activities involving about RM45 million, although those cases remain at a preliminary stage of investigation.

items.title: Jaslinda recovering well after 14-day ordeal in Perak jungle, hospital monitoring continues
items.summary: TAPAH, June 7 — Female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who was found safe yesterday after going missing for 14 days at Gunung Batu Putih, remains in stable condition but requires further treatment and monitoring at Tapah Hospital. Perak Health Director Datuk Dr Feisul Idzwan Mustapha said Jaslinda underwent a computed tomography (CT) scan at Teluk Intan Hospital early this morning as part of efforts to assess her condition. However, she still requires close monitoring and several additional examinations to ensure her recovery progresses well,” he told Bernama when contacted today.

items.title: Anwar says leadership requires patience as he defends stance amid UEC criticism
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has stressed that community leaders must have a high level of patience in facing current challenges, while remaining committed alongside the Madani Government to realise the national development agenda. He said that despite various accusations, it is the responsibility of leaders to continue fulfilling the trust given by the people. Also present were Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.

items.title: You can’t park there — woman crashes Porsche into Gurney Drive hotel after family visit
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 7 — An elderly woman accidentally stepped on the accelerator, causing the Porsche she was driving to crash into the lobby area of a hotel at Persiaran Gurney here today, an incident that has also gone viral on social media. Northeast District Police Chief ACP Abdul Rozak Muhammad said that the police received a report regarding the incident from the 61-year-old local woman at 12:05 pm and investigations revealed that the accident occurred at around 11:15 am. Earlier, several photos went viral showing a grey Porsche inside a hotel lobby, believed to have lost control before crashing into the area.

items.title: ‘Please stay in one place’: Rescued hiker Jaslinda found messages meant to save her, but days too late
items.summary: TAPAH, June 7 — The female climber who was found safe after going missing in the Gunung Batu Putih area, Jaslinda Saludin, 49, found two notes left by the search and rescue (SAR) team at Kem Air Busok during the operation. “The note was left by a forestry mountain guide (MGP) known as Hafiz while conducting a search in the Kem Air Busok area on May 30. “On that day, the search team focused on the route from Pos Musoh to Kem Air Busok and the note was left as an indication that the SAR team had passed through the area,” he said.

items.title: Upko’s application to join Sabah ruling coalition set for decision on June 18
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 7 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) is expected to decide on the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation's (Upko) application to join the coalition as a component party on June 18. He said a key condition for Upko's admission is its commitment to GRS’ four core principles: Sabah First, Sabah Forward, Sabah Prosper and Sabah United. GRS currently comprises five component parties: Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah and Parti Cinta Sabah.

items.title: Govt approves RM267m to keep Bomba on 100pc standby ‘at all times’ for emergencies
items.summary: TELUK INTAN, June 7 — The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) has allocated substantial funds to ensure the Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) remains fully prepared for emergencies at all times. Minister Nga Kor Ming said the ministry has approved RM267 million this year to upgrade equipment for search and rescue missions. Nga said the ministry will provide written details on the estimated costs of Jaslinda’s search and rescue mission.

items.title: Perikatan on the brink? PAS to decide fate of Bersatu alliance in Monday meeting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 7 — The future of the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition hangs in the balance as PAS will convene a high-stakes central working committee (CWC) meeting tomorrow night to determine its ongoing relationship with Bersatu. PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang confirmed the meeting today, when speaking to reporters following the launch of a book on the Iran-Israel war at the Kuala Lumpur International Book Fair, Free Malaysia Today reported. We will have a meeting at night,” Hadi said, addressing mounting speculation over when the party leadership would formally decide the trajectory of the PAS-Bersatu partnership.
